mentions.note: Defense contractor had strong year in 2025; benefiting from Trump administration defense budget increase to $1.5T.
Defense contractor benefiting from Denmark NATO commitments (F-35 jets, military equipment); geopolitical stability dependent; tariff risks present but defense spending supports.

Bob asks about defense contractors with reasonable dividend and long-term appeal. I like RTX (aerospace/defense environment); liked since 1978. Trump's no-buyback/no-dividend threat makes hornet's nest for many.
Presidential social media pressure on buyback practices worked; company halted share repurchases to accelerate production and capacity; stock rallied nicely; dividend safe; clear message received.
HALO trade beneficiary; helped build data centers; momentum lost as market rotated back to enterprise software.

Behind Patriot missiles, SM-3 interceptors, Tomahawk cruise missiles; US running low on Tomahawks after Iran strikes; benefits from defense and commercial aerospace; up 58% last year; best defense company.
Rare dip in defense/commercial aerospace mix; buy ahead of quarter; RTX dips tend not to last, especially in defense upcycle.
Organic revenue +9–10%, FCF +65%, all segments beat; Raytheon munitions +40% YoY; Coyote counter-UAS ramping; $271B backlog; stock down 4.4% on excellent quarter—major buy.
Aerospace maintenance/parts company getting hit hard by airline/travel pullback from Iran war; actively cutting guidance.
Down 12% post-earnings despite solid beat & demand strength. Monster buy on irrational dip. Travel/aircraft servicing demand remains robust.
Number two defense choice with large commercial business providing stability and offset to defense spending cycles.
